hcd_data.statement_of_contribution: Sonya’s Parkette has value as a small park that was created to provide the neighbourhood with greater green space for family-oriented activities, leisure, and a community meeting place. facade_description: 63 Oxford St presents a parks/open space facade within the Kensington Market HCD. The street elevation spans roughly 5.5 metres across 1 storeys and is capped by a gable, consistent with its parks/open space typology. The facade reads primarily as mixed in a open snowy parkette with chain-link fencing and colourful graffiti mural walls beyond palette, laid in running bond with light grey mortar joints. The visible opening pattern is organized as 0 window bays from lower to upper levels, using mixed. Character-defining facade elements include cornice. HCD note: Sonya’s Parkette has value as a small park that was created to provide the neighbourhood with greater green space for family-oriented activities, leisure, and a community meeting place. The property has cultural heritage value for its association with Sonya Lunansky, whom the park is named after. A
